payload.query: Woodrow Wilson opined that Congress in committees is Congress at work. In light of the reforms of the 1960s and 1970s, does this still apply? Drawing upon the literature of legislative organization and congressional behavior, explain how modern committees relate to those Wilson observed.

articles.title: Resistance to Trump Isn’t Enough
articles.abstract: Why liberals need a plan and promise to make Congress great again.
articles.snippet: Why liberals need a plan and promise to make Congress great again.

articles.title: Lee H. Hamilton, Congressman Who Helped Lead a 9/11 Inquiry, Dies at 94
articles.abstract: A moderate Democrat from Indiana for 34 years, he chaired the House Foreign Affairs and Intelligence committees and helped investigate the Iran-contra scandal and the Sept. 11 attacks.
articles.snippet: A moderate Democrat from Indiana for 34 years, he chaired the House Foreign Affairs and Intelligence committees and helped investigate the Iran-contra scandal and the Sept. 11 attacks.

articles.title: Trump Takes America’s ‘Imperial Presidency’ to a New Level
articles.abstract: In his first year back in the White House, President Trump has greatly expanded executive power while embracing the trappings of royalty in ways not seen in the modern era.
articles.snippet: In his first year back in the White House, President Trump has greatly expanded executive power while embracing the trappings of royalty in ways not seen in the modern era.

articles.title: On the Road for Women’s Rights, 110 Years Later
articles.abstract: In 1916, two suffragists drove from New York to California and back to push for the right to vote. Now, the same type of car will make the same trip to promote the Equal Rights Amendment.
articles.snippet: In 1916, two suffragists drove from New York to California and back to push for the right to vote. Now, the same type of car will make the same trip to promote the Equal Rights Amendment.

articles.title: Remember President Palmer? Exactly.

articles.title: Christopher Bond, Former Missouri Governor and U.S. Senator, Dies at 86
articles.abstract: A Republican known as Kit, he was the state’s youngest governor. When he retired from Congress after four terms, he said he didn’t want to be the state’s oldest senator.
articles.snippet: A Republican known as Kit, he was the state’s youngest governor. When he retired from Congress after four terms, he said he didn’t want to be the state’s oldest senator.

articles.title: 100 Years of the Motel
articles.abstract: In 1925, the first motel opened on a California highway, ushering in a new era of convenience and comfort for a nation falling in love with the open road.
